name: Smothered Pork Chops
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan, melt butter over medium-high heat. Stir in the 2 tablespoons flour and cook, stirring constantly with a wire whisk, until lightly browned. Stir in broth and sage, mix well, remove from heat, and set aside. This mixture will be the sauce.
2. Season pork chops with salt and pepper. Dredge each chop in seasoned flour.
3. Heat 1/4 cup lard in a large, heavy skillet over medium-high heat. Add chops and cook until browned on all sides. Transfer browned chops to an oven-proof casserole dish with cover.
4. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
5. In the same skillet, melt remaining lard over high heat. Add onions, garlic, and celery; lower heat to medium and cook, stirring frequently until onions are golden (about 8 to 10 minutes). Remove vegetables with a slotted spoon and place them over the chops in the casserole.
6. Pour off all lard from the skillet. Return skillet to low heat. Add reserved sauce, raise heat to high, bring to a boil, and cook for 1 minute, stirring vigorously and scraping the bottom of the skillet.
7. Pour sauce over chops in the casserole, cover with lid, and bake in the preheated oven for 30 minutes. Serve hot with mashed potatoes.
Serves: 6
